p1_string_of_nickels.physical_facts: Dense foliage, yellow pot. Leaves remain turgid with consistent coloration.
p1_string_of_nickels.explanatory_transformations: Stable over the 5-day sequence. No significant morphological shifts observed.
p1_string_of_nickels.visual_health_reasoning: High resilience. No signs of chlorosis or wilting; leaf orientation remains optimal.

p2_mexican_mint.physical_facts: Centralized in black pot, two primary leaves with secondary growth. Soil surface contains white mineral/perlite deposits.
p2_mexican_mint.explanatory_transformations: The white deposits (user-added) have remained stable since T-5. Leaf posture shows slight drooping compared to T-5.
p2_mexican_mint.visual_health_reasoning: Stressed. Leaf margins show persistent dehydration. The drooping is a physiological response to VPD stress, not soil moisture deficiency.

p3_pothos.physical_facts: Two leaves, white rabbit anchor (5cm) present. Soil surface shows white debris.
p3_pothos.explanatory_transformations: Leaf tip necrosis on the left leaf has remained static since T-5. No further tissue degradation.
p3_pothos.visual_health_reasoning: Stable but sensitive. The static nature of the necrosis suggests the plant has reached a temporary equilibrium with the current VPD.

p4_silver_guest.physical_facts: Small seedling near the rim of the p2/p4 shared pot.
p4_silver_guest.explanatory_transformations: Growth is minimal but consistent. No signs of etiolation.
p4_silver_guest.visual_health_reasoning: Healthy. The seedling is maintaining its structural integrity despite the shared pot environment.

biome_observations.soil_surface: Consistent presence of white mineral/perlite additives across all pots. No fungal blooms or crusting detected.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clear of debris; wiring for sensors is organized and stable.

temporal_deltas.summary: The sequence shows a period of relative stasis. The most significant change was the initial application of white material (perlite/amendment) which has been successfully integrated into the surface layer.

visual_health_inference.reasoning: While soil moisture appears adequate (based on the lack of severe wilting), the persistent leaf margin stress in p2 and p3 indicates that the ambient humidity/temperature ratio is the primary limiting factor for transpiration.

anomalies.detected: None. The white material on the soil is confirmed as a user-applied amendment per instructions.

vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently in a 'maintenance phase'. The plants are not showing signs of rapid decline, but the persistent VPD stress is preventing full recovery of the Mexican Mint (p2). The Pothos (p3) remains the most stable indicator of the current environment. No new physiological anomalies were detected during this audit.
